title: G2: Prove the mediation a-path (efficacy->learning) and b-path (learning->performance)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body: The core 'does learning behavior mediate' claim requires two justified structural links. Write the single-mediator model with X=team efficacy, M=learning behavior, Y=team performance: M = i_M + a*X + e_M ; Y = i_Y + c'*X + b*M + e_Y. (a) a-path: efficacy positively affects learning behavior (a != 0) -- confidence to seek feedback, ask for help, discuss errors, experiment (Edmondson 1999). (b) b-path: learning behavior positively affects performance NET of efficacy (b != 0) -- error correction, adaptation, coordination. Indirect (mediated) effect = a*b. Both a and b must be argued nonzero with mechanism, not assumed.
